assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Check for restrictive PDBs - This is the #1 cause' and mentions PDBs multiple times as the primary issue to investigate

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces -o wide' commands

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase!=Succeeded,status.phase!=Failed', 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep Terminating', and 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o wide | grep [NODE_NAME]'

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention checking for resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods that might indicate insufficient cluster resources

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Response provides specific kubectl patch commands to adjust PDBs: 'kubectl patch pdb [PDB_NAME] -n [NAMESPACE] --type merge -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' and similar commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE's PDB timeout behavior or notification system for blocked evictions

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Response includes surge upgrade settings with specific gcloud commands: '--max-surge=2 --max-unavailable=1' and '--max-surge=3 --max-unavailable=0'

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention webhooks or admission controllers as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Response includes monitoring commands in section 7: 'watch -n 30 'kubectl get nodes'' and 'kubectl get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep -i drain'
